title: Consumer prices rose 3.3% in March, as energy prices spiked due to Iran conflict
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Consumer prices rose 3.3% in March due to a spike in energy prices caused by the Iran conflict, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This increase took
excerpt: Consumer prices rose 3.3% in March due to a spike in energy prices caused by the Iran conflict, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This increase took the Federal Reserve further from its inflation target. The rise was driven by soaring energy costs linked to the conflict.

content.introduction: Consumer prices rose 3.3% in March due to a spike in energy prices caused by the Iran conflict, according to the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This increase took the Federal Reserve further from its inflation target. The rise was driven by soaring energy costs linked to the conflict.
body: The consumer price index increased as expected in March in response to the war in Iran, the Bureau of Labor Statistics reported Friday.
